text.data-narration: An agile process is an iterative and incremental approach to software development that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

g.text: Agile processes are designed to respond quickly to change and deliver working software in short cycles.

text.data-narration: Agile processes are iterative and incremental, with a focus on flexibility and collaboration.

g.text: Iterative and incremental, with a focus on flexibility and collaboration.

text.data-narration: Agile processes encourage continuous improvement and learning.
Extreme Programming is an agile process model that emphasizes technical practices such as pair programming, continuous integration, and testing.

g.text: XP emphasizes technical practices such as pair programming, continuous integration, and testing.

text.data-narration: The key principles of XP include pair programming, continuous integration, testing, and refactoring.

g.text: The key principles of XP include pair programming, continuous integration, testing, and refactoring.

text.data-narration: Pair programming involves two developers working together on the same code, with one developer writing the code and the other reviewing and testing it.

g.text: Pair programming involves two developers working together on the same code, with one developer writing the code and the other reviewing and testing it.

text.data-narration: Continuous integration involves integrating code changes frequently to ensure that the software is always in a stable and testable state.

g.text: Continuous integration involves integrating code changes frequently to ensure that the software is always in a stable and testable state.

text.data-narration: Scrum, Kanban, and Lean software development are examples of other agile process models.

g.text: Scrum, Kanban, and Lean software development are examples of other agile process models.

text.data-narration: In conclusion, agile processes are iterative and incremental approaches to software development that emphasize flexibility, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

g.text: In conclusion, agile processes are iterative and incremental approaches to software development that emphasize flexibility, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
